rulings.Case Title: LESLIE C MCWHIRTER VS. STEVEN A FABBRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 4, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT STEVEN FABBRO'S Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MAYO VS. ALICE TSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Obey Court Order, For Monetary Sanctions And To Compel Deposition And For Monetary Sanction; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 4, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF RICHARD MAYO'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Obey Court Order, For Monetary Sanctions And To Compel Deposition And For Monetary Sanction. Motion granted. Defendants to provide further responses, defendants to remit $1,500 in sanctions to plaintiff, and defendant to appear for deposition by October 12, 2010. Plaintiff's objection to Whitworth declaration and attached exhibits sustained. Plaintiff replied to substance of late-filed opposition which waived timeliness objection, therefore defendants' motion for relief from late filing is granted. However, defendants' late filings of September 30, 2010 are stricken as untimely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MAYO VS. ALICE TSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defts' Request For Relief From Late Filing Of Defts' Motion In Opposition To Pltf'S Motion To Compel Defts To Obey Court Order, Monetary Sanctions And To Compel Deposition For Excusable Neglect, Mistake Or Inadvertence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 4, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANTS ALICE TSE, ESCALADE CAPITAL GROUP, INNOVISTECH, L.L.C.'S Motion For Relief From Late Filing Of Defendants' Motion In Opposition To Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendants To Obey Court Order, Monetary Sanctions And To Compel Deposition For Excusable Neglect, Mistake Or Inadvertence is off calendar. Matter to be heard on Discovery calendar, and shall be considered with plaintiff's motion to compel defendants to obey court order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LOTUS GENERAL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. IMR CONTRACTORS CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Jodie Bente And Fernando Soriano And For Monetary Sanctions Against
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 4, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT IMR CONTRACTOR CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Jodie Bente And Fernando Soriano And For Monetary Sanctions. Denied. Defendant fails to demonstrate a reasonable and good faith effort to meet and confer. Defendant must pay plaintiff $1,000 as mandatory sanctions. (C.C.P. section 2023.020). =(302/CWW)
